steps.title: Choose the right model
steps.content: Doubling at a fixed interval is exponential growth with base 2. The standard form is $$M(t) = M_0 \cdot 2^{t/T},$$ where $M_0$ is the starting value and $T$ is the doubling period.

steps.title: Plug in the given values
steps.content: The account starts at $M_0 = 940$ dollars and doubles every $T = 12$ years, giving $$M(t) = 940 \cdot 2^{t/12}.$$ Quick check at $t=12$: $940 \cdot 2^1 = 1880$, which is double the original — correct.

steps.title: Evaluate the choices
steps.content:
• A. $M(t)=940\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{t/12}$ — this halves every 12 years, the opposite of doubling.
  • B. $M(t)=940\left(\frac{1}{12}\right)^{t/2}$ — decays toward zero, not doubling.
  • C. $M(t)=940(2)^{t/12}$ — doubles every 12 years as required.
  • D. $M(t)=940(12)^{t/2}$ — grows twelvefold every 2 years, far too fast.
That confirms C is correct.
